recipes.title: Run ChimeraX commands from Python
recipes.description: Use chimerax.core.commands.run when a display operation is easier or more stable as a ChimeraX command than direct API calls.
notes: This is often the best bridge between Python automation and ChimeraX display commands.
Only run command strings that you trust or construct carefully.
recipes.script:
from chimerax.core.commands import run

# The global `session` object is available in ChimeraX runscript files.
run(session, "open 1a0s")
run(session, "cartoon")
run(session, "color bychain")
run(session, "view")
session.logger.info("Opened and styled 1a0s")

recipes.title: Iterate open atomic models
recipes.description: List open AtomicStructure models and log basic atom/residue/chain counts.

official_references.title: Save images of each chain of a structure

notes: Use this as a starting point for scripts that need to inspect every loaded molecular model.
recipes.script:
from chimerax.atomic import AtomicStructure

models = [m for m in session.models if isinstance(m, AtomicStructure)]
if not models:
    session.logger.warning("No atomic structures are open")

for model in models:
    chain_ids = sorted({chain.chain_id for chain in model.chains})
    session.logger.info(
        f"#{model.id_string} {model.name}: "
        f"{model.num_atoms} atoms, {model.num_residues} residues, "
        f"{len(chain_ids)} chains ({', '.join(chain_ids)})"
    )

recipes.title: Summarize the current atom selection
recipes.description: Collect selected atoms and residues, then emit a JSON marker that an MCP caller can parse from the ChimeraX Log.
notes: The marker prefix is intentionally distinct from ChimeraX's normal log text.
recipes.script:
import json
from chimerax.atomic import selected_atoms, selected_residues

atoms = selected_atoms(session)
residues = selected_residues(session)
payload = {
    "selected_atoms": len(atoms),
    "selected_residues": len(residues),
    "residues": [str(r) for r in residues[:50]],
    "truncated": len(residues) > 50,
}
session.logger.info("CHIMERAX_MCP_RESULT_JSON=" + json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False))

recipes.title: Emit JSON for an MCP caller
recipes.description: Minimal pattern for returning structured results from a runscript by writing a marker-prefixed JSON line to the ChimeraX Log.
notes: A future Python-run tool can extract this marker automatically.
Keep payloads bounded so the MCP response remains manageable.
recipes.script:
import json

payload = {
    "status": "ok",
    "message": "structured result from ChimeraX",
    "items": [],
}
session.logger.info("CHIMERAX_MCP_RESULT_JSON=" + json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False))

recipes.title: Build a structure summary JSON payload
recipes.description: Summarize open atomic structures as JSON for downstream processing by an MCP client or LLM.

official_references.title: Inspect atomic data structures interactively in Python

notes: This produces raw JSON, not a rich report block list.
recipes.script:
import json
from chimerax.atomic import AtomicStructure

models = [m for m in session.models if isinstance(m, AtomicStructure)]
rows = []
for model in models:
    rows.append({
        "id": f"#{model.id_string}",
        "name": model.name,
        "atoms": model.num_atoms,
        "residues": model.num_residues,
        "chains": len(model.chains),
    })

payload = {
    "model_count": len(models),
    "atom_count": sum(row["atoms"] for row in rows),
    "residue_count": sum(row["residues"] for row in rows),
    "models": rows,
}
session.logger.info("CHIMERAX_MCP_RESULT_JSON=" + json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False))

recipes.title: Build a structure summary rich report payload
recipes.description: Analyze open atomic structures and emit a JSON payload shaped for the chimerax_rich_report tool.

official_references.title: Save images of each chain of a structure

notes: The emitted JSON can be passed to chimerax_rich_report after extracting the CHIMERAX_MCP_RESULT_JSON marker.
Plain text fields in chimerax_rich_report are escaped by the MCP tool.

recipes.title: Measure a CA distance between two residues
recipes.description: Look up two residues by chain ID and residue number, then compute the distance between their CA atoms.
notes: Edit chain_id_1/residue_number_1 and chain_id_2/residue_number_2 before running.

recipes.title: Find residues near selected atoms
recipes.description: Find residues with atoms within a cutoff of the current atom selection.
notes: This simple O(n*m) pattern is best for small selections; optimize for large systems.

recipes.title: Color models by chain from Python
recipes.description: Use ChimeraX commands from Python to apply a common visualization style and color chains distinctly.

official_references.title: Copy chain colors from one structure to another

notes: Visualization state is often best expressed as ChimeraX commands even inside a Python script.
recipes.script:
from chimerax.core.commands import run

run(session, "hide atoms")
run(session, "cartoon")
run(session, "color bychain")
run(session, "lighting soft")
run(session, "view")
session.logger.info("Applied cartoon and chain coloring")

recipes.title: Return script errors as structured JSON
recipes.description: Wrap a script body in try/except and emit either a success or error JSON marker for an MCP caller.
notes: Use this template when the caller needs machine-readable failures rather than only traceback text.
